title: Odisha B.Ed Hall Ticket 2026

detail.title: Odisha B.Ed Hall Ticket 2026
detail.short_info: Odisha B.Ed Hall Ticket 2026 will be released today, 27 May 2026, for the SAMS Odisha B.Ed entrance exam on 31 May 2026 and B.H.Ed/M.Ed on 3 June 2026. Download your Odisha B.Ed admit card 2026 from ssbodisha.ac.in using roll number and date of birth, and check details mentioned, required documents and exam‑day instructions here.

content.text: The Odisha State Selection Board (SSB Odisha) will release the Odisha B.Ed Hall Ticket 2026 today, 27 May 2026, for the Teacher Education entrance tests. As per the official schedule under SAMS Odisha, the admit card download for B.Ed/B.H.Ed/M.Ed Entrance Test 2026 starts on 27 May 2026 from 2:00 PM onwards.

The Odisha B.Ed Entrance Exam 2026 (Arts & Science) will be held on 31 May 2026, while B.H.Ed (Hindi) and M.Ed entrance exams will be conducted on 3 June 2026 at various centres across the state. Candidates who have registered successfully through SAMS Odisha can download their hall tickets from the SSB Odisha admit card portal.

sections.heading: Odisha B.Ed Entrance Exam 2026 - Key Details

rows: Odisha B.Ed / B.H.Ed / M.Ed Entrance Test 2026
State Selection Board (SSB Odisha) on behalf of the Higher Education Department, Odisha, through SAMS
Hall Ticket to be Released on 27 May 2026
Admit Card Section on SSB Odisha Website
Roll Number and Date of Birth

sections.heading: How to Download Odisha B.Ed Hall Ticket 2026 (Once Out Today)

content.text: After the link is activated today, follow these steps to download your Odisha B.Ed Hall Ticket 2026:

items: Visit the official SSB Odisha website ssbodisha.ac.in.
On the homepage, go to the “Admit Card / Hall Ticket” section or find the link titled “Download Hall Ticket (Admit Card) for B.Ed/B.H.Ed/M.Ed Admission Test‑2026”.
Click on the B.Ed Entrance Exam 2026 admit card link; a login page will appear.
Enter your Roll Number and Date of Birth, then submit the captcha code (if shown).
Click on Submit / Download; your Odisha B.Ed Entrance Exam 2026 hall ticket will be displayed on the screen.
Check all details (name, exam date, timing, centre) and download and print a coloured A4‑size copy of the hall ticket; keep extra copies for safety.

content.text: If the website is slow right after release (2 PM onwards), candidates should wait a few minutes and try again instead of repeatedly refreshing the page.

sections.heading: Details Mentioned on Odisha B.Ed Hall Ticket 2026

content.text: The Odisha B.Ed admit card 2026 will contain the following important details:

items: Candidate’s name
Roll number / Hall ticket number
Date of birth
Candidate’s photograph and signature
Exam name: Odisha B.Ed/B.H.Ed/M.Ed Entrance Test 2026
Exam date and time (31 May 2026 for B.Ed; 3 June 2026 for B.H.Ed & M.Ed)
Reporting time and gate‑closing time
Exam venue name and complete address
Exam centre code
Exam day instructions and guidelines

content.text: Candidates must ensure that all these details are correct and clearly printed; in case of any discrepancy, they should contact the authorities through the helpline given on SAMS/SSB websites.

sections.heading: Documents Required with Odisha B.Ed Hall Ticket 2026

content.text: For smooth entry into the exam hall, candidates must carry the following on the day of the Odisha B.Ed Entrance Exam 2026:

items: A coloured printout of the Odisha B.Ed Hall Ticket 2026 on A4‑size sheet.
A valid original photo identity proof, such as Aadhaar Card, PAN Card, Voter ID, Passport, Driving Licence or any other government‑issued ID.
Two recent passport‑size photographs, preferably the same as uploaded in the application form (recommended in many guides).

content.text: Candidates who fail to produce the hall ticket and valid ID proof will not be allowed to appear for the examination.

sections.heading: Exam‑Day Instructions for Odisha B.Ed Entrance Exam 2026

content.text: To ensure a smooth exam experience, candidates should follow these Odisha B.Ed 2026 exam‑day guidelines:

items: Reach the examination centre well before the reporting time mentioned on the hall ticket; late entry after gate‑closing time will not be permitted.
Carry only necessary items such as hall ticket, photo ID, photographs and permitted stationery; avoid bringing bags and unnecessary materials to the centre.

Do not carry mobile phones, smart watches, calculators (if not allowed), Bluetooth devices, books, notebooks or any electronic gadgets inside the exam hall; possession of such items may lead to disqualification.
Sit in the allotted seat and follow the instructions given by invigilators and those printed on the question booklet/OMR sheet; any unfair practice will result in cancellation of candidature.
Preserve your Odisha B.Ed hall ticket even after the exam, as it may be required during result, counselling and admission stages.
